t: LLC or S-Corp — Which Saves You More Money? | JK Accounting Group Skip to the calculator JK Accounting GroupTAX · BOOKKEEPING · CFO Book a free call Free calculator · 1 minute LLC or S‑Corp — which saves you more money? The way your business is set up changes how much tax you pay. Answer two simple questions and see which setup could save you more — and by how much. By JK Accounting Group · For business owners in the US Built with JuliaFounder · speaks your language Try it with your numbers Nothing is saved and no email is needed. You will see your answer right away. What does your business earn in a year? $ After your business costs, before you pay yourself. Please enter how much your business earns. What is a fair salary for your work? $ If you hired someone to do your job, what would you pay them for the year? An S‑Corp owner must take a fair salary first. Please enter a fair salary (it cannot be more than what the business earns). Show my savings Takes about a minute. Your estimate As an LLC, you pay Self‑employment tax on your whole profit. As an S‑Corp, you pay The same tax, but only on your salary. See if this is right for you — book a free call → A 20‑minute call. No cost, no pressure. What this really means In plain words, so you can decide with your eyes open. Self‑employment tax is 15.3%. It pays for Social Security and Medicare when you work for yourself. Everyone who runs a business pays it. An S‑Corp splits your money in two. You take a fair salary, and the rest is business profit. You pay that 15.3% on the salary — but not on the rest. That is where the saving comes from. It is not free. An S‑Corp costs a little more to run — payroll and an extra tax return, usually a few thousand dollars a year. That is why it is worth checking your real numbers before you change anything. Hi, I'm Julia. Founder, JK Accounting GroupEnglish · Russian · Ukrainian I help business owners who came to the US and now have to learn a whole new set of tax rules. We become your finance team — bookkeeping, taxes, and staying right with the IRS — so you can focus on your business. This calculator gives you a quick idea. When you are ready to check your real numbers, I would be glad to look at them with you. Want us to check your exact numbers? Send us your details and we will get back to you — in English, Russian, or Ukrainian. Or book a free 20‑minute call right away. A real person who speaks your language. We tell you if an S‑Corp is worth it — even if the answer is "not yet." No cost and no pressure. Prefer WhatsApp? Message us here Your name * Email * Phone or WhatsApp Optional. We will use whatever is easiest for you. Best language for you English Russian Ukrainian Send my details Or book a call now Keep more of what your business earns. We will look at your real numbers and tell you the smart move — clearly, in your language. Book a free call → See our other free tools Miami · Fort Lauderdale · Online General information, not tax advice. This calculator gives a simple estimate using 2026 tax rates. Your real numbers depend on your full situation. Please talk to us before you make any decision. JK Accounting GroupTAX · BOOKKEEPING · CFO Miami · Fort Lauderdale · Online Bookkeeping · Taxes · CFO — in English, Russian & Ukrainian Book a free call // (function () { "use strict"; var WAGE_BASE = 184500; // 2026 Social Security wage base — see TAX-FIGURES-TO-VERIFY.md var form = document.getElementById("calc"); var result = document.getElementById("result"); var fProfit = document.getElementById("f-profit"); var fSalary = document.getElementById("f-salary"); function seTaxOnProfit(profit) { var base = profit * 0.9235; return 0.124 * Math.min(base, WAGE_BASE) + 0.029 * base; } function ficaOnSalary(salary) { return 0.124 * Math.min(salary, WAGE_BASE) + 0.029 * salary; } form.addEventListener("submit", function (e) { e.preventDefault(); var profit = LM.num(document.getElementById("profit").value); var salary = LM.num(document.getElementById("salary").value); var ok = true; fProfit.classList.remove("invalid"); fSalary.classList.remove("invalid"); if (!(profit > 0)) { fProfit.classList.add("invalid"); ok = false; } if (!(salary > 0) || (profit > 0 && salary > profit)) { fSalary.classList.add("invalid"); ok = false; } if (!ok) { result.hidden = true; return; } var llc = seTaxOnProfit(profit); var scorp = ficaOnSalary(salary); var savings = llc - scorp; document.getElementById("r-llc").textContent = LM.money(llc); document.getElementById("r-scorp").textContent = LM.money(scorp); var headline = document.getElementById("r-headline"); var explain = document.getElementById("r-explain"); var note = document.getElementById("r-note"); headline.classList.remove("warn"); if (savings >= 1500) { headline.innerHTML = 'You could save about ' + LM.money(savings) + ' a year.'; explain.textContent = "As an LLC you pay self-employment tax on your whole profit. As an S-Corp you pay it only on your salary, not on the rest. That difference is your saving."; note.innerHTML = "Remember: an S-Corp costs a little more to run — payroll and an extra tax return, usually a few thousand dollars a year. Your real saving is a bit lower. We help you check if it is worth it for you."; } else if (savings > 0) { headline.classList.add("warn"); headline.innerHTML = 'You might save about ' + LM.money(savings) + ', but an S‑Corp may cost more than that.'; explain.textContent = "At these numbers the saving is small. An S-Corp costs extra to run — payroll and an extra tax return. It may not be worth it yet, but that can change as you grow."; note.innerHTML = "Not sure? This is exactly the kind of thing we can check for you in a quick call — including the best time to switch."; } else { headline.classList.add("warn"); headline.innerHTML = 'An S‑Corp may not save you money yet.'; explain.textContent = "With this salary and profit, an S-Corp does not lower your self-employment tax. That is normal for many businesses — it often makes sense only once profits grow."; note.innerHTML = "Good to know: we can tell you the profit level where an S-Corp starts to pay off for you. That is a quick question for us."; } LM.showResult(result); }); })(); //
